title: Neocracca heterantha
taxonomy: Neocracca heterantha is classified within the family Rosaceae, which comprises flowering plants commonly known as rose family. This classification is based on its morphological characteristics and genetic relationships with other members of the family. As a member of the Rosaceae family, Neocracca heterantha is an angiosperm, a type of flowering plant that produces seeds within fruits or flowers. Its classification also places it within the order Rosales, a group of flowering plants that are primarily woody and have five-petaled flowers. Neocracca heterantha's classification is a way of organizing and categorizing its evolutionary relationships with other plants.

It is said to possess anti-inflammatory properties, making it useful for treating conditions such as arthritis and other inflammatory diseases. Additionally, Neocracca heterantha is believed to have antimicrobial and antioxidant properties, which may help protect against infections and oxidative stress.
